meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Project Template to Ship Fast
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on your coding projects. Learn to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to finish your first real-world app with confidence.
content.answer_block: The 'can't finish' cycle stems from scope creep and architectural paralysis. By using the Antigravity project template, you enforce a modular structure that limits feature bloat. Paired with Claude Code, you automate boilerplate and focus purely on core logic, ensuring your MVP moves from concept to deployment in days, not months.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: the repository is initialized, the excitement is electric, and then—the silence of a half-finished README sets in. You are trapped in the 'tutorial purgatory,' where the gap between knowing how to code and actually shipping a functional SaaS product feels like an insurmountable canyon. Every unfinished project is a weight on your confidence, stalling your transition from student to senior-level builder.

But what if you could bypass the friction of structural setup? The Antigravity project template isn't just a boilerplate; it is a battle-tested architecture designed for rapid iteration. By combining this framework with the raw power of Claude Code, you turn your terminal into an engine of high-velocity development, finally giving you the momentum to push your code to production.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Base
steps.description: Establish the foundational directory structure that enforces separation of concerns, preventing the 'spaghetti code' trap early on.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new project using the Antigravity standard template with a focus on a modular feature-based architecture.

steps.title: Define the Schema and State
steps.description: Use Claude Code to map out your database requirements, ensuring your data model is robust enough to support your MVP features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project goals and generate a normalized database schema that fits within the Antigravity data-layer conventions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate prisma schema based on project requirements'

steps.title: Implement the Core Logic Hook
steps.description: Focus on the single most valuable feature of your app. Don't worry about CSS or auth yet; build the engine.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer function that handles the primary business logic, following the Antigravity dependency injection pattern.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'implement service layer for core feature'

steps.title: Automate Interface Generation
steps.description: Use the template's UI components to rapidly build your frontend, keeping it lightweight and functional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive view for the core service created in step 3 using the project's pre-defined component library.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate frontend view for core feature'

steps.title: Deploy to Production Environment
steps.description: Finalize your build and push to a live environment to solidify the psychological win of finishing your project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the deployment pipeline for the current project structure and optimize for production performance.

pro_tips: Always commit your progress in small, atomic chunks to maintain a clean history that serves as a portfolio artifact.
Treat the Antigravity template as a rigid constraint; do not modify the core directory structure until you have shipped at least three projects.
Use Claude Code's 'review' command on your own code to find potential performance bottlenecks before they become technical debt.
Ignore 'perfect' styling in your first iteration; focus exclusively on the functionality that solves the core problem for your user.
Build a 'Kill Switch' feature: define the absolute minimum requirement to call the project 'done' and refuse to add new features until that milestone is reached.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, document the 'lessons learned' in a blog post, share the repo on your portfolio, and identify one small feature to iterate on next week.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting unfinished projects stall your career growth. You have the tools, the template, and the roadmap to ship your first production-ready application. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
